title: Antibiotic Stewardship Trainings | Antibiotic Prescribing and Use | CDC
text:
stewardship trainings.
CDC's antibiotic stewardship courses 
This online training course includes multiple modules with free continuing education (CE) that can be taken in any order.
Course objectives: 
Describing the role of the healthcare team in antibiotic stewardship. 
Updating healthcare professionals on current antimicrobial resistance threats. 
Encouraging open discussion among healthcare professionals and patients about appropriate antibiotic prescribing. 
Course highlights include content on: 
Antimicrobial resistance threats in the United States 
Risks and benefits of antibiotics 
Benefits and principles of antibiotic stewardship 
Epidemiology of antibiotic use in the United States and advancement in clinical settings 
Communication training for clinicians to improve outpatient antibiotic prescribing and use 
CDC's Antibiotic Stewardship Courses 
Access trainings.
Click here 
CDC recognizes everyone plays an important role in improving antibiotic use. Physicians, nurse practitioners, physician associates, certified health education specialists, nurses, pharmacists and public health practitioners with a master's degree in public health are eligible to receive free continuing education.
This course fulfills Improvement Activities (IA) Patient Safety and Practice Assessment (PSPA)_23 and PSPA_24 under the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) Merit-Based Incenti
